name: Makepad
description: Live-coding IDE and in-house framework, unique DSL and rendering

license.comment: Crates.io metadata lists 'MIT OR Apache-2.0'; the LICENSE file in the repo is the MIT text with copyright 'Makepad B.V.'

crates-io.comment: makepad-widgets is the top-level framework crate application developers depend on (per Makepad Book and robius.rs tutorials). The older 'makepad' crate on crates.io is not the current entry point.

documentation.comment: Official 'Makepad Book' is hosted at makepad.rs (book.makepad.rs redirects there).

first-release.comment: GitHub repository created 2019-02-20. The current top-level crate makepad-widgets was first published 2022-10-20 (v0.3.0), but Makepad has been a public project since 2019 under earlier crate names.

maintenance-status.comment: Repo pushed 2026-04-22 (two days before this pass); dev branch receives frequent commits.

maturity.comment: 1.0 released May 2025. The HN release thread acknowledged 'rough edges and missing bits', so 'mature' would overstate; 'stable' reflects the 1.0 milestone.

corporate-backing.comment: LICENSE copyright is held by Makepad B.V. (Netherlands). Core developers include Rik Arends; no public details on commercial model. Related Project Robius is a community effort that builds on Makepad.

implementation.comment: No C/C++ toolkit is wrapped. Linux requires system libs (wayland, X11, EGL, Mesa) but those are platform glue, not a widget toolkit; shader compiler (MPSL) and renderer are all Rust.

rendering-mode.comment: Makepad documents itself as a hybrid: retained widget tree with shader-based GPU rendering, plus a 'dual immediate mode' for data-driven widget drawing via begin/drawitem/end.

reactivity-model.comment: Widgets emit actions that are dispatched to handler methods via the MatchEvent trait; widgets are mutated directly in handle_event. No fine-grained signals, no virtual-DOM diffing, no Elm-style pure update function.

thread-model.comment: Cx context and widget tree are driven on the main thread; background work uses framework-provided async primitives (cx.http_request etc.).

renderer.comment: Backends: Metal (macOS/iOS), DirectX 11 (Windows), OpenGL (Linux/Android), WebGL (WASM). Ships a custom shader language (MPSL) that is translated to each platform's shading language. Not built on wgpu.

gpu-acceleration.comment: Shader-based GPU rendering is the core architectural premise.

hidpi-support.comment: Native Metal/DX11/OpenGL backends with DPI-aware rendering; Makepad Studio ships for Retina-class displays.

vector-rendering.comment: Vector/font/image engines are part of the platform stack; the 'vector' example demonstrates resolution-independent drawing.

custom-shaders.comment: User-written shaders in MPSL are the primary styling mechanism; the 'shader' example is dedicated to this.

embedded-custom-rendering.comment: The entire framework is a custom GPU pipeline; examples like 'arracing', 'fractal zoomer', and 'shader' embed custom shaders and GPU content as widget content.

linux-wayland.comment: Linux build deps include libwayland-dev and wayland-protocols alongside X11 packages; Makepad has a native Wayland path via EGL.

android.comment: Ironfish synthesizer is the flagship Android example.

web-wasm.comment: Compiles to WASM with a WebGL renderer; --wasm-opt and --brotli tooling is provided.

embedded-no-std.comment: No no_std or microcontroller story; the framework assumes a full Rust std and a platform event loop.

authoring-style.comment: UI and styling are authored in Live DSL, which is embedded inside Rust via the live_design! macro. Event handling stays in imperative Rust.

hot-reload.comment: Live styling: applications recompile and execute new Live DSL at runtime without restarting, with no Rust recompile required for style/layout edits.

layout-system.comment: Custom nested box model executed in 'semi-immediate mode', with Fill/Fit sizing and flow/spacing directives. Not flexbox, not CSS grid; closest match here is 'auto' (widget-driven).

styling-approach.comment: Styles live in Live DSL blocks and work similarly to CSS but with Makepad's own syntax and shader hooks (MPSL).

theme-switching.comment: No documented runtime light/dark theme switching primitive; theme_desktop_dark files exist but a user-triggered switch mechanism is not documented in the Book.

accessibility.comment: boringcactus' 2025 survey reports Windows Narrator cannot see window chrome or content. No AccessKit integration, no native a11y layer. Contributors on HN flagged a11y as a known gap.

internationalization.comment: Bundles Chinese/emoji font sub-crates for glyph coverage, but no built-in i18n framework (no locale loader, no RTL-aware layout primitives documented).

widget-breadth.comment: Common widgets are present: button, label, text_input, check_box, slider, drop_down, popup_menu, tabs, splitter, portal_list (virtualized), scroll_bar, image, markdown, html. No data-grid or tree widget. Reasonable middle-of-pack stdlib.

data-grid-table.comment: No table/grid widget in the widgets crate; portal_list gives virtualized single-column lists.

rich-text-markdown.comment: markdown.rs and html.rs widgets exist; pulldown-cmark is a direct dep of makepad-widgets.

tree-outline.comment: No tree/outline widget; would be hand-rolled with portal_list + nested views.

native-menus.comment: popup_menu and menu widgets are custom-drawn with Makepad's own rendering, not native OS menu bars.

file-dialogs.comment: No first-party native file dialog widget or documented rfd integration found. Likely works via user-added crates but not verified.

charts-plotting.comment: A 'charts' example exists but is custom-drawn per app; no reusable charting widget in the widgets crate.

custom-widgets.comment: Custom widgets via #[derive(Widget)] and live_design! templates are central to the framework, but the DSL is under-documented per the 2025 boringcactus survey, so the onboarding cost is real.

async-runtime-compatible.comment: Makepad provides its own async primitives (e.g. cx.http_request) and event loop. No first-party tokio or async-std integration documented.

system-tray.comment: No tray-icon API documented or shipped with the framework.

notifications.comment: No native OS notification API documented in widgets or platform crates.

clipboard.comment: text_input and text_selection widgets suggest basic copy/paste within text fields, but no public clipboard read/write API was found in docs. Not verified.

drag-and-drop.comment: No native OS drag-and-drop API documented. Not verified.

embedded-webview.comment: Makepad renders entirely on its own GPU stack; no webview embedding.

multi-window.comment: Examples and templates all ship single-window apps with one top-level Window in live_design!. Could not confirm or rule out multiple top-level windows from public docs.

binary-size.comment: No reliable public measurement for a hello-world Makepad release binary. Refusing to guess.

startup-time.comment: No reliable public measurement. Makepad ships GPU shader precompile and demos feel snappy, but no reproducible cold-start number.

memory-footprint.comment: No reliable public measurement of idle RSS for a minimal app.

idle-redraw.comment: Framework has event-driven redraw and an explicit next_frame trigger; behavior depends on app (animations vs static UI). Could not conclusively classify as on-demand vs continuous.

dependency-count.comment: makepad-widgets lists 12 direct deps (most of them internal makepad-* crates); total transitive cargo tree for a minimal app lands in the ~50-200 range typical for a GPU GUI stack. Not measured exactly on this pass.

documentation-quality.comment: Makepad Book exists with guides and architecture pages, and Robius tutorials exist. But the Live DSL reference is thin and 2025 survey called out 'no documentation' for the load-bearing macro DSL. Middling overall.

api-ergonomics.comment: Derive macros (#[derive(Widget)]) and live_design! are idiomatic for Makepad, but developers must learn a second language (Live DSL + MPSL) and manage explicit draw/handle_event functions. Functional but non-trivial mental model.

examples-count.comment: 25 example directories on dev branch (counter, uizoo, todo, charts, browser, aichat, maps, shader, xr, etc.).

interactive-playground.comment: No hosted online playground found. WASM demos exist per-example (e.g. Ironfish, Fractal Zoomer) but there is no central REPL.

ide-tooling.comment: Makepad Studio is a first-party authoring/inspection app for Live DSL; no LSP, formatter, or general-purpose editor plugin is shipped for external IDEs.

dev-tools-inspector.comment: Makepad Studio provides live UI editing and inspection tooling; the README also highlights 'AI automation inside Studio to control and inspect UI'.

crates-io-downloads.comment: All-time downloads of makepad-widgets as of 2026-04-24.

notable-production-users.value: Robrix (Matrix client, Project Robius), Moly (local-LLM client), Ironfish synthesizer demo, Makepad Studio itself
notable-production-users.comment: Robrix (pre-alpha) is the most visible third-party app. No large commercial shipping app publicly uses Makepad as of this research pass.

community-size.comment: Discord exists; contributor activity is concentrated around a core Makepad team plus Project Robius collaborators. Not a large open community.

ecosystem-crates.comment: Almost all makepad-* crates are first-party sub-crates of the Makepad repo. No meaningful third-party theme/widget/integration ecosystem on crates.io yet.
